# Fetch Apple’s public key to verify token signatures

**Web Service Endpoint**

Fetch Apple’s public key to verify ID token and server notification signatures.

## Discussion

If successful, the HTTP status code is 200 (OK) and the [doc://com.apple.signinwithapple/documentation/SigninwithAppleRESTAPI/JWKSet/Keys-data.dictionary] object contains Apple’s public key. For more information, see [doc://com.apple.signinwithapple/documentation/signinwithapple/verifying-a-user] and [doc://com.apple.signinwithapple/documentation/signinwithapple/processing-changes-for-sign-in-with-apple-accounts#Decode-and-validate-the-notifications].



## Generating and revoking tokens

- **Creating a client secret**: Generate a signed token to identify your client application.
- **Token validation**: Validate an authorization grant code delivered to your app to obtain tokens, or validate an existing refresh token.
- **Token revocation**: Invalidate the tokens and associated user authorizations for a user when they are no longer associated with your app.
